project: GitHub Pages Coding Blog with Game & Data Feed

tasks.title: Initialize GitHub Pages Repository & Structure
tasks.rationale: Foundation for all other tasks; enables subsequent file creation and Pages deployment.
risk_flags: Ensure GitHub Pages is enabled in repo settings (Settings > Pages > main branch)

tasks.title: Design Homepage Layout & Information Architecture
tasks.rationale: Define visual structure before coding; enables parallel CSS/content creation; expandable for future assignments.
risk_flags: Ensure navigation scalability (design for 5+ future pages)

tasks.title: Implement Homepage HTML & Base CSS Framework
tasks.rationale: Creates the main landing page with responsive design; foundation for navigation links.
risk_flags: Use semantic HTML5; ensure CSS is vendor-prefixed for cross-browser compatibility

tasks.title: Build Navigation Component & Content Sections
tasks.rationale: Adds navigation links to Pac-Man game and arXiv feed; enables user access to all content.
risk_flags: Links must be relative paths: href='/pacman/index.html' and href='/arxiv.html'

tasks.title: Design Pac-Man Game Architecture & State Management
outputs: entity-hierarchy.json (Pac-Man, Ghost, Rose, Heart classes structure)
tasks.rationale: Define game module boundaries (entities, maze, collision, AI, projectiles) before coding to ensure smooth implementation.
risk_flags: Rose spawn timing must be random but frequent enough to test power-ups
Heart-ghost collision detection is critical for power-up gameplay

tasks.title: Implement Core Game Engine (Maze, Collision, Movement)
outputs: js/maze.js (maze layout, wall collision, pellet system)
js/entities.js (Pac-Man, Ghost, Rose, Heart class definitions)
js/input.js (keyboard controls: arrow keys, game start/pause)
tasks.rationale: Core game loop foundation; must complete before Pac-Man movement & AI logic.
risk_flags: Maze layout should be a 2D array; consider making it configurable
Collision detection needs to handle both entity-wall and entity-entity cases

tasks.title: Implement Pac-Man Movement & Ghost AI Behavior
outputs: js/ghost-ai.js (simple pathfinding/chase algorithm for each ghost)
js/game-loop.js (main update/render loop, game state management)
tasks.rationale: Implements playable Pac-Man mechanics and ghost coordination; prerequisite for power-ups.
risk_flags: Ghost AI: consider simple A* or Manhattan distance chase; avoid complex algorithms
Game loop timing: use requestAnimationFrame for smooth 60fps

tasks.title: Implement Power-Up System (Rose & Heart Projectiles)
outputs: js/projectiles.js (Heart entities, trajectory, lifetime, ghost elimination)
js/scoring.js (score tracking, bonus points for hearts/ghosts)
tasks.rationale: Implements Valentine's-themed core features (roses, hearts); fulfills Problem 2 unique requirement.
risk_flags: Rose spawn should be time-based + random location (not on walls/pellets)
Heart projectiles must travel in Pac-Man's facing direction continuously during power-up
Power-up duration must be balanced (suggest 5-8 seconds)

tasks.title: Create Pac-Man Game HTML Page & Visual Styling
outputs: pacman/index.html (game canvas, controls instructions, score display)
pacman/styles.css (Valentine's theme: pink/red colors, heart emojis, responsive)
tasks.rationale: Creates playable web page for game; links from homepage (P1c dependency).
risk_flags: Canvas must be responsive but maintain aspect ratio
Include game over screen with restart button
Display lives, score, power-up timer

tasks.title: Design arXiv Data Pipeline Architecture
outputs: arxiv-pipeline.md (data flow: API → fetch script → JSON → HTML → deploy)
tasks.rationale: Define data pipeline (fetch → transform → generate → deploy) before implementation; clarifies script responsibilities.
risk_flags: arXiv API: rate limit ~1 req/3 sec; use search_query parameter effectively
Midnight trigger: use cron='0 0 * * *' in GitHub Actions (UTC)
Error handling: workflow must not fail silently; add logging

tasks.title: Implement arXiv Fetcher & Data Pipeline Scripts
outputs: scripts/fetchArxiv.js (Query arXiv API by keyword, parse XML, extract metadata)
scripts/generateFeedHTML.js (Transform papers.json → arxiv.html with card layout)
data/papers.json (structured paper data: title, authors, abstract, pdf_url, date)
tasks.rationale: Core data fetching & transformation; must complete before GitHub Actions workflow.
risk_flags: arXiv API returns XML; use parser library or regex carefully
Handle errors: timeout (30s), no results, malformed data
Store papers.json for GitOps workflow (commit to repo)

tasks.title: Create GitHub Actions Workflow for Auto-Update
tasks.rationale: Automates nightly paper updates; enables hands-off deployment to GitHub Pages.
risk_flags: GitHub Actions token: use GITHUB_TOKEN (auto-provided) for commits
Skip CI on workflow commits: add [skip ci] in git commit message or use immutable workflow trigger
Test workflow locally with act tool before deploying

tasks.title: Create arXiv Feed Display Page
outputs: arxiv.html (paper cards, expandable abstracts, PDF links, back-to-home link)
styles/arxiv.css (responsive grid, card styling, dark theme alignment)
tasks.rationale: Displays fetched papers in user-friendly format; links from homepage.
risk_flags: Page must work with dynamically updated papers.json
Include metadata: fetch timestamp, paper count, last update time
PDF links should target arXiv.org directly (don't host PDFs)

tasks.title: Integration Testing & Final README Documentation
outputs: README.md (complete: project overview, setup instructions, Copilot CLI usage, file structure)
DEPLOYMENT.md (steps to enable GitHub Pages, test locally, customize arXiv keywords)
tasks.rationale: Documentation for graders + future maintainability; validates all components work together.
risk_flags: README must list ALL Copilot CLI prompts used and what worked/required iteration
Include screenshots or gifs of game & paper feed if possible
Add troubleshooting section (common issues: GitHub Pages not deploying, cron job not triggering)

execution_notes.parallelization: Wave 2 (P1a, P2a, P3a) can all run in parallel after P0 completes. These are architectural tasks that don't depend on each other. Similarly, Wave 3 (P1b, P2b, P3b) are all parallel implementations.
execution_notes.critical_path: P0 → P1a → P1b → P1c → P2a → P2b → P2c → P2d → P2e is the longest chain. However, P3 pipeline can run in parallel.
execution_notes.agent_sequencing: Start with all architects (P1a, P2a, P3a), then distribute code-generators (P1b, P1c, P2b, P2c, P2d, P3c, P3d) and devops tasks (P0, P3b).
human_checkpoints: After P1a: Review homepage wireframe with course requirements
After P2a: Test game architecture pseudocode for feasibility
After P3a: Validate workflow design with GitHub Actions documentation
After P2e: Play game locally to verify core mechanics before submission

high_risk: P2c: Ghost AI pathfinding must be performant (avoid infinite loops)
P3b: arXiv API rate limiting; ensure scripts respect request throttling
P3c: GitHub Actions workflow token permissions; ensure safe commit practices
medium_risk: P2d: Power-up duration balance; may require playtesting iteration
P3a: Cron scheduling in UTC; verify midnight trigger matches user timezone expectations
low_risk: P1b: CSS cross-browser compatibility (use standard properties)
